expectations.text: system.md written at docs/architecture/system.md
system.md carries a C4 diagram (Mermaid C4 canonical, or an ASCII fallback)
system.md has a strategic-DDD bounded-context map
system.md follows the arc42 subset (>=4 sections incl. Crosscutting Concepts + Quality)
system.md references the spine by REQ-ID (traceability, not copied requirement prose)
ADR registry: adr/README.md index + >=1 MADR ADR with a checkable Rule (Binds/Prevents/Rule)
expectations.evidence: index=yes; adr files=['ADR-001.md', 'ADR-002.md', 'ADR-003.md', 'ADR-004.md']; MADR fields=True; Rule+Binds/Prevents=True

expectations.text: ADR IDs are contiguous from ADR-001 with no gaps/dupes (max+1 allocation)
A feature spec references the sprint's REQs and carries a Verification Contract (method + pass-criterion)
expectations.evidence: specs=['auth.md', 'digest.md', 'standups.md', 'team-management.md']; sprint-REQ refs=['REQ-001', 'REQ-004', 'REQ-005', 'REQ-006', 'REQ-007', 'REQ-008']; VC with method=True

expectations.text: REQ coverage: every in-scope sprint REQ is covered by >=1 feature spec
DM coverage (forward): every required 02-manifest DM-ID is covered by a feature spec
amendment-log.json is valid JSON with an 'amendments' array
Reconciler isolation: a context attestation is recorded (fresh-spawner proxy; transcript check is manual)
system.md §10: every quality scenario names an executable fitness function (or deferred:<why>) — no prose-only quality claims (D1)
system.md § Test Strategy (core): a named test shape (pyramid/trophy/honeycomb) + a flake-quarantine policy (ticket + owner + fix-or-remove SLA) (D2)
A feature spec carries an Observability row — what it logs/emits + what "healthy" means (D3)
Migration contract (D4, conditional): N/A — no destructive data-model change in the feature specs
expectations.evidence: no destructive DDL → migration row not required (additive/initial schema)

expectations.text: system.md § Threats considered (D6): >=1 boundary-derived threat, each with a mitigation (a constraint / ADR / accepted-risk) — the Four Questions over the C4 trust boundaries
Validity: the datastore was genuinely underspecified ([NEEDS CLARIFICATION], preserved in the constraint file or the amendment source_quote)
expectations.evidence: datastore now='PostgreSQL 16+ (managed, client-server relational;'; marker in file|source_quote=True

expectations.text: Flesh-out: a Tier-2 amendment row resolves the underspecified datastore decision, resolved_by an ADR
expectations.evidence: tier-2 rows=1; resolving-datastore=1; with ADR=1; dispositions=['approved']

expectations.text: Concrete decision: the realization (system.md / an ADR Decision) names a concrete datastore the envelope lacked
Both, or neither: the Datastore line itself was fleshed out — marker cleared from the field, a concrete datastore named on the line
expectations.evidence: post-run Datastore line='PostgreSQL 16+ (managed, client-server relational; EU region) — resolved via Tie'

expectations.text: Verify-live ADR citation (S18, conditional): N/A — no verify-live tech declared
